𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Seven — Part One

The Book of Yovelim

YOVELIM

The Book of Jubilees — Part One

Chapters 1 through 25

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 1 YHWH Speaks to Moshe on Sinai — The Heavenly Tablets Revealed

1:1 And it came to pass in the first year of the exodus of the children of Yisra'EL out of Egypt, in the third month, on the sixteenth day of the month, that YHWH spoke to Moshe, saying: Come up to me on the mountain, and I will give you two tables of stone of the law and of the commandments, which I have written, that you may teach them.

1:2 And Moshe went up into the mountain of YHWH, and the glory of YHWH abode on Mount Sinai, and a cloud overshadowed it six days.

1:3 And he called to Moshe on the seventh day out of the midst of the cloud, and the appearance of the glory of YHWH was like a flaming fire on the top of the mountain.

1:4 And Moshe was on the mountain forty days and forty nights, and YHWH taught him the earlier and the later history of the division of all the days of the law and of the testimony.

1:5 And he said: Incline your heart to every word which I shall speak to you on this mountain, and write them in a book in order that their generations may see how I have not forsaken them for all the evil which they have wrought in transgressing the covenant which I establish between me and you for their generations this day on Mount Sinai.

1:6 And thus it will come to pass when all these things come upon them, that they will recognize that I am more righteous than they in all their judgments and in all their actions, and they will recognize that I have been truly with them.

1:7 And do you write for yourself all these words which I declare to you this day, for I know their rebellion and their stiff neck, before I bring them into the land of which I swore to their fathers, to Avraham and to Yitzhak and to Yaakov, saying: Unto your seed will I give a land flowing with milk and honey.

1:8 And they shall eat and be satisfied, and they will turn to strange deities, to such as cannot deliver them from any of their tribulation, and this witness shall be heard for a witness against them.

1:9 For they will forget all my commandments, even all that I command them, and they will walk after the nations, and after their uncleanness, and after their shame, and will serve their deities, and these will prove unto them an offence and a tribulation and an affliction and a snare.

1:10 And many will perish and they will be taken captive, and will fall into the hands of the enemy, because they have forsaken my ordinances and my commandments, and the festivals of my covenant, and my Sabbaths, and my holy place which I have hallowed for myself in their midst, and my tabernacle, and my sanctuary, which I have hallowed for myself in the midst of the land, that I should set my name upon it, and that it should dwell there.

1:11 And they will make to themselves high places and groves and graven images, and they will worship, each his own graven image, so as to go astray, and they will sacrifice their children to demons, and to all the works of the error of their hearts.

1:12 And I will send witnesses unto them, that I may witness against them, but they will not hear, and will slay the witnesses also, and they will persecute those who seek the law, and they will abrogate and change everything so as to work evil before my eyes.

1:13 And I will hide my face from them, and I will deliver them into the hand of the nations for captivity, and for a prey, and for devouring, and I will remove them from the midst of the land, and I will scatter them among the nations.

1:14 And they will forget all my law and all my commandments and all my judgments, and will go astray as to new moons, and Sabbaths, and festivals, and jubilees, and ordinances.

1:15 And after this they will turn to me from among the nations with all their heart and with all their soul and with all their strength, and I will gather them from among all the nations, and they will seek me, so that I shall be found of them, when they seek me with all their heart and with all their soul.

1:16 And I will reveal to them abounding peace with righteousness, and I will remove the plant of uprightness, with all my heart and with all my soul, and they shall be for a blessing and not for a curse, and they shall be the head and not the tail.

1:17 And I will build my sanctuary in their midst, and I will dwell with them, and I will be their Creator and they shall be my true and righteous children.

1:18 And I will not forsake them nor fail them, for I am YHWH their Creator.

1:19 And Moshe fell on his face and prayed and said: O YHWH my Creator, do not forsake your people and your inheritance, so that they should wander in the error of their hearts, and do not deliver them into the hands of their enemies, the nations, lest they should rule over them and cause them to sin against you.

1:20 Let your mercy, O YHWH, be lifted up upon your people, and create in them an upright spirit, and let not the spirit of Beliyar rule over them to accuse them before you, and to ensnare them from all the paths of righteousness, so that they may perish from before your face.

1:21 But they are your people and your inheritance, which you have delivered with your great power from the hands of the Egyptians — create in them a clean heart and a holy spirit, and let them not be ensnared in their sins from henceforth until eternity.

1:22 And YHWH said unto Moshe: I know their contrariness and their thoughts and their stiffneckedness, and they will not be obedient till they confess their own sin and the sin of their fathers.

1:23 And after this they will turn to me in all uprightness and with all their heart and with all their soul, and I will circumcise the foreskin of their heart and the foreskin of the heart of their seed, and I will create in them a holy spirit, and I will cleanse them so that they shall not turn away from me from that day unto eternity.

1:24 And their souls will cleave to me and to all my commandments, and they will fulfil my commandments, and I will be their father and they shall be my children.

1:25 And they shall all be called children of the living Creator, and every angel and every spirit shall know, yea, they shall know that these are my children, and that I am their father in uprightness and righteousness, and that I love them.

1:26 And do you write down for Moshe the words from the first creation until my sanctuary has been built among them for all eternity.

1:27 And YHWH will appear to the eyes of all, and all shall know that I am the Creator of Yisra'EL and the father of all the children of Yaakov, and King on Mount Zion for all eternity. And Zion and Yerushalayim shall be holy.

1:28 And the angel of the presence spoke to Moshe according to the word of YHWH, saying: Write the complete history of the creation, how in six days YHWH completed all his works and all that he created, and kept Shabbat on the seventh day and hallowed it for all ages, and appointed it as a sign for all his works.

1:29 For on the first day he created the heavens which are above and the earth and the waters and all the spirits which serve before him — the angels of the presence, and the angels of sanctification, and the angels of the spirit of fire and the angels of the spirit of the winds, and the angels of the spirit of the clouds, and of darkness, and of snow and of hail and of hoar frost, and the angels of the voices and of the thunder and of the lightning, and the angels of the spirits of cold and of heat, and of winter and of spring and of autumn and of summer, and of all the spirits of his creatures which are in the heavens and on the earth.

TEXTUAL NOTES

[DSS] 4QJuba (4Q216) preserves substantial portions of Chapter 1 in Hebrew. The Book of Yovelim is attested in fifteen separate manuscripts from Qumran — more than any non-Torah book except Tehillim and Yesha'yahu. The Qumran community regarded Yovelim as authoritative scripture — the Damascus Document explicitly cites it as a source of halakhic authority.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full and treats Yovelim as canonical scripture within the Beta Israel Ethiopian tradition.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Book of Yovelim presents itself as the revelation given to Moshe on Sinai through the angel of the presence. The revelation is the heavenly record — the account of creation, the history of the patriarchs, and the covenant laws — as recorded on the heavenly tablets. Yovelim's primary theological contribution is the organization of all of history into jubilee periods — units of forty-nine years, seven weeks of years — establishing that all historical time is organized according to the covenant calendar of Vayikra 25.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1:20 — let not the spirit of Beliyar rule over them to accuse them before you. The angel Beliyar — the prince of darkness, the accuser — appears in Yovelim as the cosmic opponent of the covenant people. He is the same figure called the prince of darkness in the War Scroll (1QM) and the father of lies in the Damascus Document.

CREATION AND THE FIRST JUBILEE — CHAPTERS 2 THROUGH 4

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 2 The Six Days of Creation — The Shabbat Covenant

2:1 And the angel of the presence spoke to Moshe according to the word of YHWH, saying: Write the complete history of the creation, how in six days YHWH completed all his works and all that he created, and kept Shabbat on the seventh day and hallowed it for all ages, and appointed it as a sign for all his works.

2:2 For on the first day he created the heavens which are above and the earth and the waters and all the spirits which serve before him — the angels of the presence, and the angels of sanctification, and the angels of the spirit of fire and the angels of the spirit of the winds, and the angels of the spirit of the clouds, and of darkness, and of snow and of hail and of hoar frost, and the angels of the voices and of the thunder and of the lightning.

2:3 And on the second day he created the firmament in the midst of the waters, and the waters were divided on that day — half of them went up above and half of them went down below the firmament that was in the midst over the face of the whole earth. And this was the only work YHWH created on the second day.

2:4 And on the third day he commanded the waters to pass from off the face of the whole earth into one place, and the dry land to appear.

2:5 And the waters did so as he commanded them, and they retired from off the face of the earth into one place outside of this firmament, and the dry land appeared.

2:6 And on that day he created for them all the seas according to their separate gatherings, and all the rivers, and the gatherings of the waters in the mountains and on all the earth, and all the lakes, and all the dew of the earth, and the seed which is sown, and all sprouting things, and fruit-bearing trees, and trees of the wood, and the garden of Eden, in Eden, and all plants after their kind.

2:7 These four great works YHWH created on the third day.

2:8 And on the fourth day he created the sun and the moon and the stars, and set them in the firmament of the heaven, to give light upon all the earth, and to rule over the day and the night, and divide the light from the darkness.

2:9 And YHWH appointed the sun to be a great sign on the earth for days and for Sabbaths and for months and for feasts and for years and for Sabbaths of years and for jubilees and for all seasons of the years.

2:10 And it divides the light from the darkness and for prosperity, so that all things may prosper which shoot and grow on the earth.

2:11 These three kinds YHWH made on the fourth day.

2:12 And on the fifth day he created great sea monsters in the depths of the waters, for these were the first things of flesh that were created by his hands, the fish and everything that moves in the waters, and everything that flies, the birds and all their kind.

2:13 And the sun shone over them to prosper them, and over everything that was on the earth, everything that shoots out of the earth, and all fruit-bearing trees, and all flesh.

2:14 These three kinds he created on the fifth day.

2:15 And on the sixth day he created all the animals of the earth, and all cattle, and everything that moves on the earth.

2:16 And after all this he created man, a man and a woman created he them, and gave him dominion over all that is on the earth, and in the seas, and over everything that flies, and over beasts and over cattle, and over everything that moves on the earth, and over the whole earth, and over all this he gave him dominion.

2:17 And these four kinds he created on the sixth day.

2:18 And there were altogether two and twenty kinds, and YHWH finished all his works on the sixth day — all that he had created in heaven and on earth, and in the seas and in the abysses, and in the light and in the darkness, and in everything.

2:19 And he gave us a great sign, the Shabbat day, that we should work six days, but keep Shabbat on the seventh day from all work.

2:20 And all the angels of the presence, and all the angels of sanctification, these two great classes — he has bidden us to keep the Shabbat with him in heaven and on earth.

2:21 And he said unto us: Behold, I will separate unto myself a people from among all the peoples, and these shall keep the Shabbat day, and I will sanctify them unto myself as my people, and will bless them — as I have sanctified the Shabbat day and do sanctify it unto myself, even so will I bless them, and they shall be my people and I will be their Creator.

2:22 And I have chosen the seed of Yaakov from among all that I have seen, and have written him down as my first-born son, and have sanctified him unto myself for ever and ever — and I will teach them the Shabbat day, that they may keep Shabbat thereon from all work.

2:23 And thus he created therein a sign in accordance with which they should keep Shabbat with us on the seventh day, to eat and to drink, and to bless him who has created all things as he has blessed and sanctified unto himself a peculiar people above all peoples, and that they should keep Shabbat together with us.

2:24 And he caused his commands to ascend as a sweet savor acceptable before him all the days.

2:25 There were two and twenty heads of mankind from Adam to Yaakov, and two and twenty kinds of work were made until the seventh day — this is blessed and holy, and the former also is blessed and holy, and this one serves with that one for sanctification and blessing.

2:26 And to this one YHWH granted that it should be the most excellent of all, that there should rest on it the spirit of holiness.

2:27 And he said unto us: You anoint the head with the oil of gladness and with the spirit of holiness.

2:28 And there was not one of all of them that kept Shabbat, save those to whom YHWH spoke on the mountain of Sinai with regard to Yisra'EL.

2:29 And he allowed the other peoples to keep Shabbat from the day they transgressed the commandment through death, to cause them to be a people of the destroyer.

2:30 And be careful to observe the Shabbat, and observe it with all glory, because it is more holy than any day.

2:31 And he who desecrates it shall surely die, and he who does any work thereon, he who is defiled on it, and he who lies with a woman, and he who says he will do something on it, that he will set out on a journey thereon in regard to any buying or selling, and whoever draws water thereon which he had not prepared for himself on the sixth day, and whoever takes up any burden to carry it out of his tent or out of his house, he shall die.

2:32 You shall do no work whatever on the Shabbat day save that you have prepared for yourself on the sixth day, so as to eat, and to drink, and to rest, and to keep Shabbat from all work on that day, and to bless YHWH your Creator who has given you a day of festival and a holy day, and a day of the holy kingdom for all Yisra'EL is this day among their days forever.

TEXTUAL NOTES

[DSS] 4QJuba (4Q216) and 4QJubb (4Q217) preserve substantial portions of Chapter 2. The Shabbat law of Yovelim aligns with the strict Qumran Shabbat halakhah as described in the Damascus Document Column 10-11.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2:9 — YHWH appointed the sun to be a great sign for days and Sabbaths and months and feasts and years and jubilees. The sun — not the moon — governs the covenant calendar. This solar calendar theology is the foundation of the Qumran community's calendar dispute with the Jerusalem Temple authorities who used a lunisolar calendar.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2:20 — the angels of the presence and the angels of sanctification keep Shabbat with YHWH in heaven. The Shabbat is a cosmic covenant that the highest orders of angels observe alongside humanity. When Yisra'EL keeps Shabbat, they join a heavenly liturgy already in progress. This theology is the foundation for the Qumran Songs of the Sabbath Sacrifice (4QShirShab).

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 3 Adam and Chavah in the Garden — The Fall and the Garments

3:1 And on the six days of the second week we brought, according to the word of YHWH, unto Adam all the beasts, and all the cattle, and all the birds, and everything that moves on the earth, and everything that moves in the water, according to their kinds, and according to their types — the beasts on the first day, the cattle on the second day, the birds on the third day, and all that which moves on the earth on the fourth day, and that which moves in the water on the fifth day.

3:2 And Adam named them all by their respective names, and as he called them, so was their name.

3:3 And on these five days Adam saw all these, male and female, according to every kind that was on the earth, but he was alone and found no helpmeet for himself.

3:4 And YHWH said unto us: It is not good that the man should be alone — let us make a helpmeet for him.

3:5 And YHWH our Creator caused a deep sleep to fall upon him, and he slept, and he took for the woman one rib from among his ribs, and this rib was the origin of the woman from among his ribs, and he built up the flesh in its stead, and built the woman.

3:6 And he awaked Adam out of his sleep and on awaking he rose on the sixth day, and he brought her to him, and he knew her and said unto her: This is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh — she shall be called woman, because she was taken from man.

3:7 Therefore shall man and his wife be one, and therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and cleave to his wife, and they shall be one flesh.

3:8 In the first week was Adam created, and the rib — his wife — in the second week. And after Adam had completed forty days in the land where he had been created, we brought him into the garden of Eden to till and keep it.

3:9 And his wife they brought in on the eightieth day, and after this she entered into the garden of Eden.

3:10 And for this reason the commandment is written on the heavenly tablets in regard to her that gives birth: if she bears a male, she shall remain in her uncleanness seven days according to the first week of days, and thirty and three days shall she remain in the blood of her purifying, and she shall not touch any hallowed thing, nor enter into the sanctuary, until she accomplishes these days which are enjoined in the case of a male child.

3:11 But in the case of a female child she shall remain in her uncleanness two weeks of days, according to the two weeks of Adam and his rib, and sixty and six days in the blood of her purification, and they will be in all eighty days.

3:12 And when she had completed these eighty days we brought her into the garden of Eden, for it is holier than all the earth besides and every tree that is planted in it is holy.

3:13 Therefore, there was ordained regarding her who bears a male or a female child the statute of those days that she should touch no hallowed thing, nor enter into the sanctuary from the days till those days for the male or female child are accomplished.

3:14 This is the law and testimony which was written down for Yisra'EL, in order that they should observe it all the days.

3:15 And in the first week of the first jubilee, Adam and his wife were in the garden of Eden for seven years tilling and keeping it, and we gave him work and we instructed him to do everything that is suitable for tillage.

3:16 And he tilled the garden, and was naked and knew it not, and was not ashamed, and he protected the garden from the birds and beasts and cattle, and gathered its fruit, and ate, and put aside the residue for himself and for his wife.

3:17 And after the completion of the seven years, which he had completed there, seven years exactly, and in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the serpent came and approached the woman.

3:18 And the serpent said to the woman: Has YHWH commanded you, saying, You shall not eat of every tree of the garden?

3:19 And she said to it: Of all the fruit of the trees of the garden YHWH has said unto us: Eat — but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden YHWH has said unto us: You shall not eat thereof, neither shall you touch it, lest you die.

3:20 And the serpent said unto the woman: You shall not surely die — for YHWH knows that on the day you shall eat thereof, your eyes will be opened, and you will be as deities, knowing good and evil.

3:21 And the woman saw the tree that it was agreeable and pleasant to the eye, and that its fruit was good for food, and she took thereof and ate.

3:22 And when she had first covered her shame with fig leaves, she gave thereof to Adam and he ate, and his eyes were opened, and he saw that he was naked.

3:23 And he took fig leaves and sewed them together, and made an apron for himself, and covered his shame.

3:24 And YHWH cursed the serpent, and was wroth with it forever.

3:25 And he was wroth with the woman also, because she hearkened to the voice of the serpent, and did eat — and he said unto her: I will greatly multiply your sorrow and your pains — in sorrow you shall bring forth children, and your return shall be unto your husband, and he shall rule over you.

3:26 And to Adam also he said: Because you have hearkened unto the voice of your wife, and have eaten of the tree of which I commanded you that you should not eat thereof, cursed be the ground for your sake — thorns and thistles shall it bring forth to you, and you shall eat your bread in the sweat of your face, till you return to the earth from which you were taken — for earth you are, and to earth shall you return.

3:27 And he made for them coats of skin, and clothed them, and sent them forth from the Garden of Eden.

3:28 And on that day on which Adam went forth from the Garden, he offered as a sweet savor an offering, frankincense, galbanum, and stacte, and spices in the morning with the rising of the sun from the day when he covered his shame.

3:29 And on that day was closed the mouth of all beasts, and of cattle, and of birds, and of whatever walks, and of whatever moves, so that they could no longer speak, for they had all spoken one with another with one lip and with one tongue.

3:30 And he sent out of the Garden of Eden all flesh that was in the Garden of Eden, and all flesh was scattered according to its kinds, and according to its types unto the places which had been created for them.

3:31 And to Adam alone did he give the wherewithal to cover his shame, of all the beasts and cattle.

3:32 On this account, it is prescribed on the heavenly tablets as touching all those who know the judgment of the law, that they should cover their shame, and should not uncover themselves as the nations uncover themselves.

TEXTUAL NOTES

[DSS] 4QJubc (4Q218) and 4QJubd (4Q219) preserve portions of Chapter 3. The specific chronological details reflect Yovelim's characteristic jubilee-calendar precision in dating every event in the patriarchal narrative.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3:28 — on the day Adam went forth from the Garden he offered frankincense, galbanum, and stacte. Adam's first act outside Eden is a burnt offering using the same aromatic substances that will constitute the Mishkan incense formula — Shemot 30:34. Yovelim establishes that the covenant sacrificial system did not originate at Sinai. Adam practiced it from the first day of his exile.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3:29 — on that day was closed the mouth of all beasts so that they could no longer speak, for they had all spoken one with another with one lip and one tongue. Before the expulsion, all creatures spoke with one language. After the fall, communication across species became impossible. The linguistic unity of creation was a pre-fall condition.

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 4 The Genealogy from Adam to Noakh — Chanokh's Translation

4:1 And in the third week in the second jubilee, Qa'in slew Hevel because YHWH accepted Hevel's sacrifice but Qa'in's sacrifice he did not accept.

4:2 And he slew him in the field, and his blood cried from the ground to heaven, complaining because he had slain him.

4:3 And YHWH reproved Qa'in because of Hevel, because he had slain him, and he made him a fugitive on the earth because of the blood of his brother, and he cursed him upon the earth.

4:4 And on this account it is written on the heavenly tablets: Cursed is he who smites his neighbor treacherously, and let all who have seen and heard say: So be it — and the man who has seen and not declared it, let him be accursed as the other.

4:5 For this reason we announce when we come before YHWH our Creator all the sin which is committed in heaven and on earth, and in light and in darkness, and everywhere.

4:6 And Adam and his wife mourned for Hevel four weeks of years, and in the fourth year of the fifth week they became joyful, and Adam knew his wife again, and she bore him a son, and he called his name Sheth, for he said: YHWH has raised up a second seed for us on the earth instead of Hevel — for Qa'in slew him.

4:7 And in the sixth week he begat his daughter Azura.

4:8 And Qa'in took Awan his sister to be his wife, and she bare him Hanokh at the close of the fourth jubilee. And in the first year of the first week of the fifth jubilee, houses were built on the earth, and Qa'in built a city, and called its name after the name of his son Hanokh.

4:9 And Adam knew Chavah his wife and she bore yet nine sons.

4:10 And in the fifth week of the fifth jubilee Sheth took Azura his sister to be his wife, and in the fourth year of the sixth week she bore him Enosh.

4:11 He began to call on the name of YHWH on the earth.

4:12 And in the seventh jubilee in the third week Enosh took Noam his sister to be his wife, and she bore him a son in the third year of the fifth week, and he called his name Kenan.

4:13 And at the close of the eighth jubilee Kenan took Mualeleth his sister to be his wife, and she bore him a son in the ninth jubilee, in the first week in the third year of this week, and he called his name Mahalale'el.

4:14 And in the second week of the tenth jubilee Mahalale'el took unto him to wife Dinah, the daughter of Barakiel the daughter of his father's brother, and she bore him a son in the third week in the sixth year, and he called his name Yered, for in his days the angels of YHWH descended on the earth, those who are named the Watchers, that they should instruct the children of men, and that they should do judgment and uprightness on the earth.

4:15 And in the eleventh jubilee Yered took to himself a wife, and her name was Barakah, the daughter of Rasuyyal a daughter of his father's brother, in the first week in the first year of this week, and she bore him a son in the fifth week, in the fourth year of the jubilee, and he called his name Chanokh.

4:16 And he was the first among men that are born on earth who learnt writing and knowledge and wisdom, and who wrote down the signs of heaven according to the order of their months in a book, that men might know the seasons of the years according to the order of their separate months.

4:17 And he was the first to write a testimony, and he testified to the sons of men among the generations of the earth, and recounted the weeks of the jubilees, and made known to them the days of the years, and set in order the months and recounted the Sabbaths of the years as we made them known to him.

4:18 And what was and what will be he saw in a vision of his sleep, as it will happen to the children of men throughout their generations until the day of judgment — he saw and understood everything, and wrote his testimony, and placed the testimony on earth for all the children of men and for their generations.

4:19 And in the twelfth jubilee, in the seventh week thereof, he took to himself a wife, and her name was Edna, the daughter of Danel, the daughter of his father's brother, and in the sixth year in this week she bore him a son and he called his name Metushelakh.

4:20 And he was moreover with the angels of YHWH these six jubilees of years, and they showed him everything which is on earth and in the heavens, the rule of the sun, and he wrote down everything.

4:21 And he testified to the Watchers, who had sinned with the daughters of men — for these had begun to unite themselves, so as to be defiled, with the daughters of men, and Chanokh testified against them all.

4:22 And he was taken from amongst the children of men, and we conducted him into the Garden of Eden in majesty and honor, and behold there he writes down the condemnation and judgment of the world, and all the wickedness of the children of men.

4:23 And on account of it YHWH brought the waters of the flood upon all the land of Eden — for there he was set as a sign and that he should testify against all the children of men, that he should recount all the deeds of the generations until the day of condemnation.

4:24 And he burnt the incense of the sanctuary, even sweet spices acceptable before YHWH, on the mountain.

4:25 For YHWH has four places on the earth — the Garden of Eden, and the mountain of the East, and this mountain on which you are this day, Mount Sinai, and Mount Zion which will be sanctified in the new creation for a sanctification of the earth — through it will the earth be sanctified from all its guilt and its uncleanness throughout the generations of the world.

4:26 And in the fourteenth jubilee Metushelakh took unto himself a wife, Edna the daughter of Azriel, the daughter of his father's brother, in the third week, in the first year of this week, and he begat a son and called his name Lamekh.

4:27 And in the fifteenth jubilee in the third week Lamekh took to himself a wife, and her name was Betenos the daughter of Barakiel the daughter of his father's brother, and in this week she bore him a son and he called his name Noakh, saying: This one will comfort me for my trouble and all my work, and for the ground which YHWH has cursed.

4:28 And at the close of the nineteenth jubilee, in the seventh week in the sixth year thereof, Adam died, and all his sons buried him in the land of his creation, and he was the first to be buried in the earth.

4:29 And he lacked seventy years of one thousand years, for one thousand years are as one day in the testimony of the heavens and therefore was it written concerning the tree of knowledge — on the day that you eat thereof you shall die. For this reason he did not complete the years of this day, for he died during it.

4:30 At the close of this jubilee Qa'in was killed after him in the same year — for his house fell upon him and he died in the midst of his house, and he was killed by its stones, for with a stone he had killed Hevel, and by a stone was he killed in righteous judgment.

4:31 For this reason it has been ordained on the heavenly tablets: With the instrument with which a man kills his neighbor with the same shall he be killed — after the manner that he wounded him, in like manner shall they deal with him.

4:32 And in the twenty-fifth jubilee Noakh took to himself a wife, and her name was Emzara, the daughter of Rake'el, the daughter of his father's brother, in the first year in the fifth week, and in the third year thereof she bore him Shem, in the fifth year thereof she bore him Ham, and in the first year in the sixth week she bore him Yefet.

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 5 The Watchers and the Flood — The Covenant with Noakh

5:1 And it came to pass when the children of men began to multiply on the face of the earth and daughters were born unto them, that the angels of YHWH saw them on a certain year of this jubilee, that they were beautiful to look upon, and they took themselves wives of all whom they chose, and they bare unto them sons and they were giants.

5:2 And lawlessness increased on the earth and all flesh corrupted its way, alike men and cattle and beasts and birds and everything that walks on the earth — all of them corrupted their ways and their orders, and they began to devour each other, and lawlessness increased on the earth and every imagination of the thoughts of all men was thus evil continually.

5:3 And YHWH looked upon the earth, and behold it was corrupt, and all flesh had corrupted its orders, and all that were upon the earth had wrought all manner of evil before his eyes.

5:4 And he said that he would destroy man and all flesh upon the face of the earth which he had created.

5:5 But Noakh found grace before the eyes of YHWH.

5:6 And against the angels whom he had sent upon the earth, he was exceedingly wroth, and he gave commandment to root them out of all their dominion, and he bade us to bind them in the depths of the earth, and behold they are bound in the midst of them, and are kept separate.

5:7 And against their sons went forth a commandment from before his face that they should be smitten with the sword, and be removed from under heaven.

5:8 And he said: My spirit shall not always abide on man — for they also are flesh and their days shall be one hundred and twenty years.

5:9 And he sent his sword into their midst that each should slay his neighbor, and they began to slay each other till they all fell by the sword and were destroyed from the earth.

5:10 And their fathers were witnesses of their destruction, and after this they were bound in the depths of the earth forever, until the day of the great condemnation, when judgment is executed on all those who have corrupted their ways and their works before YHWH.

5:11 And he destroyed all from their places, and there was not left one of them whom he judged not according to all their wickedness.

5:12 And he made for all his works a new and righteous nature, so that they should not sin in their whole nature forever, but should be all righteous each in his kind always.

5:14 And there is nothing in heaven or on earth, or in light or in darkness, or in Sheol or in the depth, or in the place of darkness, which is not judged — all their judgments are ordained and written and engraved.

5:15 In regard to all he will judge, the great according to his greatness, and the small according to his smallness, and each according to his way.

5:16 And he is not one who will regard the person of any, nor is he one who will receive gifts, if he says that he will execute judgment on each — if one gave everything that is on the earth, he will not regard the gifts or the person of any, nor accept anything at his hands, for he is a righteous judge.

5:17 And of the children of Yisra'EL it has been written and ordained: If they turn to him in righteousness he will forgive all their transgressions and pardon all their sins.

5:18 It is written and ordained that he will show mercy to all who turn from all their guilt once each year.

5:19 And as for all those who corrupted their ways and their thoughts before the flood, no man's person was accepted save that of Noakh alone, for his person was accepted in behalf of his sons, whom he saved from the waters of the flood on his account — for his heart was righteous in all his ways, as it was commanded regarding him, and he had not departed from anything that was ordained for him.

5:20 And YHWH said that he would destroy everything which was upon the earth, both men and cattle, and beasts, and fowls of the air, and that which moves on the earth.

5:21 And he commanded Noakh to make him an ark, that he might save himself and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives, and all the cattle and beasts and birds, and everything that moves on the earth.

5:22 And Noakh made the ark in all respects as he commanded him, in the twenty-seventh jubilee of years, in the fifth week in the fifth year thereof.

5:23 And he entered in the sixth year thereof, in the second month, on the new moon of the second month, till the sixteenth — he and all that we brought to him, into the ark, and YHWH closed it from without on the seventeenth evening.

5:24 And YHWH opened seven flood-gates of heaven, and the mouths of the fountains of the great deep, seven mouths in number.

5:25 And the flood-gates began to pour down water from the heaven forty days and forty nights, and the fountains of the deep also sent up waters, until the whole world was full of water.

5:26 And the waters increased upon the earth — fifteen cubits did the waters rise above all the high mountains, and the ark was lifted up above the earth, and it moved upon the face of the waters.

5:27 And the water prevailed on the face of the earth five months — one hundred and fifty days.

5:28 And the ark went and rested on the top of Lubar, one of the mountains of Ararat.

5:29 And on the new moon in the fourth month the fountains of the abysses of the deep were closed and the flood-gates of heaven were restrained — and on the new moon of the seventh month all the mouths of the abysses of the earth were opened, and the water began to descend into the deep below.

5:30 And on the new moon of the tenth month the tops of the mountains were seen, and Noakh was glad.

5:31 And on this account he ordained them for himself as feasts for a memorial forever, and thus are they ordained.

5:32 And at the new moon of the first month, and at the new moon of the fourth, and at the new moon of the seventh, and at the new moon of the tenth, these are the days of remembrance, and the days of the seasons in the four divisions of the year.

[DSS] 2QJub (2Q19) and 3QJub (3Q5) preserve portions of Chapter 12. The prayer of Avram and the night astronomical observation are attested in the Cave manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12:12-14 — Avram observes the stars at night to determine the year's rainfall character, then stops and says: all the signs of the stars and the moon and sun are in the hand of YHWH — why do I search them out? Avram begins as a practitioner of the astral divination that Kainam found carved on rock in Chapter 8 — the Watcher knowledge that survived the Flood. But in the middle of his astronomical observation he has the insight that YHWH governs all the signs he is studying. The student of astral omens becomes the worshiper of the one who controls what the omens reflect.

[DSS] 3QJub (3Q5) and 11QJub (11Q12) preserve portions of Chapter 13.

Yovelim's treatment of Malkizedek is deliberately brief — he appears, accepts the tithe, and does not become a theological center. He is present as the legitimate priest of the Most High in the land of Kena'an before the full establishment of the Levitical priesthood, but he is not elevated beyond what the Torah text itself says.

14:6 And he said unto him: I am YHWH that brought you out of Ur of the Chaldees, to give you the land of the Kena'anites to take possession of it forever.

[DSS] 11QJub (11Q12) preserves portions of Chapter 14. Yovelim places the covenant between the pieces on the same date as Shavuot — connecting all major covenant events to the same calendar point.

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 15 The Covenant of Circumcision — The Eternal Sign

15:3 And YHWH appeared to him, and said unto him: I am YHWH the Almighty, walk before me and be you perfect.

15:8 For the father of many nations have I made you, and I will make you very great, and I will make you into nations, and kings shall come forth from you.

15:11 And YHWH said unto Avraham: And as for you, keep my covenant, you and your seed after you, and circumcise you every male among you.

15:12 And you shall circumcise the flesh of your foreskin, and it shall be a sign of an everlasting covenant between me and you.

15:13 And the child on the eighth day you shall circumcise, every male throughout your generations, he that is born in the house, or bought with money of any foreigner, who is not of your seed.

15:15 And the uncircumcised male who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin on the eighth day, that soul shall be cut off from his people, for he has broken my covenant.

15:24 This law is for all the generations forever, and there is no circumcision of the days, and no omission of one day out of the eight days, for it is an eternal ordinance, ordained and written on the heavenly tablets.

15:25 And everyone who is born, the flesh of whose foreskin is not circumcised on the eighth day, belongs not to the children of the covenant which YHWH made with Avraham, but to the children of destruction.

15:26 For all the angels of the presence and all the angels of sanctification have been so created from the day of their creation, and before the angels of the presence and the angels of sanctification he has sanctified Yisra'EL, that they should be with him and with his holy angels.

15:31 But over Yisra'EL he did not appoint any angel or spirit, for he alone is their ruler, and he will preserve them and require them at the hand of his angels and his spirits, and at the hand of all his powers, in order that he may preserve them and bless them, and that they may be his and he may be theirs from henceforth forever.

TEXTUAL NOTES

[DSS] 4QJubf (4Q221) and 4QJubh (4Q223-224) preserve substantial portions of Chapter 15, reflecting the Qumran community's intense interest in circumcision theology.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 15 in full.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:26 — all the angels of the presence and all the angels of sanctification have been created circumcised from the day of their creation. The highest angelic orders were created with circumcision as their native condition. When Yisra'EL circumcises their sons they are conforming to the angelic standard, not establishing a new human institution.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15:30-31 — over all other nations YHWH placed spirits in authority to lead them astray, but over Yisra'EL he appointed no angel or spirit — he alone is their ruler. This is the most direct statement of Yovelim's theology of the nations, connecting to Devarim 32:8-9 DSS reading: when YHWH divided the nations he set their boundaries according to the number of the sons of The Creator, but YHWH's own portion is his people.

The feast of covenant renewal is not arbitrary — it is the appointed time built into creation's structure for the making and remembering of covenants.

YOVELIM — CHAPTER 25 Yaakov Refuses the Daughters of Kena'an — The Bethel Dream

25:1 And in the second year of this week in this jubilee, Rivkah called Yaakov her son, and spoke unto him, saying: My son, do not take to thyself a wife from the daughters of Kena'an, as Esav your brother, who took to himself two wives from the daughters of Kena'an, and they have embittered my soul with all their unclean deeds — for all their deeds are fornication and lust, and there is no righteousness with them, for their deeds are evil.

25:11 And Yaakov departed from the well of the oath to go to Haran, in the first year of the second week of the forty-sixth jubilee, and he came to Luz on the mountains, that is Bethel.

25:12 And he dreamed that night, and behold a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven, and the angels of YHWH were ascending and descending on it.

25:13 And YHWH stood above it and spoke to Yaakov, and said: I am YHWH, the Creator of Avraham your father and the Creator of Yitzhak — the land whereon you sleep, to you will I give it, and to your seed after you.

25:16 And Yaakov awoke from his sleep, and said: Surely YHWH is in this place, and I knew it not.

25:17 And he was afraid, and said: How dreadful is this place — this is none other but the house of YHWH, and this is the gate of heaven.

25:18 And Yaakov arose early in the morning, and took the stone that he had put under his head, and set it up as a pillar, and poured oil upon the top of it, and called the name of that place Bethel.

25:20 So that I come again to my father's house in peace — then shall YHWH be my Creator, and this stone, which I have set for a pillar, shall be YHWH's house, and of all that you shall give me I will surely give the tenth unto you.

TEXTUAL NOTES

[DSS] 4QJubh (4Q223-224) preserves portions of Chapter 25. The Bethel vision and Yaakov's vow are attested in the Cave manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 25 in full.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:1 — Rivkah commands Yaakov not to take a wife from the daughters of Kena'an because all their deeds are fornication and lust. Yovelim's endogamy principle — marriage within the covenant family exclusively — is the central social-covenant law that governs all the patriarchal marriages. The principle is rooted in the territorial covenant from Chapter 9. Intermarriage with Kena'anites is the social dimension of the territorial covenant violation.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 25:20 — Yaakov vows to give a tenth of all that YHWH gives him. Yaakov's tithe vow at Bethel is the third tithe-giving act in Yovelim after Avraham's tithe to Malkizedek and Avraham's instruction to Yitzhak in Chapter 21. The tithe is a patriarchal covenant practice observed by all three generations before Sinai.
